content: Is there any way to use LTO on rust_binder.o so that C can get inlined into that object file without changing the rest of the object files?
I'm having some problems with Rust getting inlined into C objects, which causes the C compilation units to have BTF debugging information that the kernel's BPF loader doesn't understand.
I have a hack for exactly that
You could use that as a starting point
uhh, does that work for built-in modules?
This patch restricts LTO to Rust modules only
For a quick test, you could mangle the link flags for just your module

Any chance I could get a crash course in what this does?

+# TODO: Only llvm-link when CLANG_LTO_FULL=y
+# TODO: Does not work for modules containing assembly files
 quiet_cmd_ld_multi_m = LD [M]  $@
-      cmd_ld_multi_m = $(LD) $(ld_flags) -r -o $@ @$< $(cmd_objtool)
+      cmd_ld_multi_m = if [ -n "$(LLVM_LINK_FIX_$@)" ]; then \
+               $(LLVM_LINK) -o $(patsubst %.o,%.ll.o,$@) $(shell cat $< | xargs); $(LD) $(ld_flags) -r -o $@ $(patsubst %.o,%.ll.o,$@) $(cmd_objtool); \
+               else $(LD) $(ld_flags) -r -o $@ @$< $(cmd_objtool); fi

Andreas Hindborg said:

There are ccflags-y, ccflags-remove-y, CFLAGS_* etc for that https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/kbuild/makefiles.txt

Wouldn't I need something to change the rustc flags instead? Is there a thing for rust flags in makefiles?
Actually, I guess I'm just asking for the idea of LTOing helpers into Rust like we've discussed many times before ...
